prerequisites: A Pylon account with API access at https://app.usepylon.com.
A generated Pylon API token (the api_token credential) for the Bearer Authorization header.
Optionally decide on a start_date to bound the earliest records to sync; if omitted, the connector uses its default behavior.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: Pylon uses Bearer token authentication. Supply your Pylon API token as the connector's api_token secret. The connector sends it in the HTTP header as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Generate the token in the Pylon dashboard under Settings > API tokens (https://app.usepylon.com/settings/api-tokens). Actions performed with a token are attributed to that token's name in your activity logs.

setupSteps.title: Create a Pylon API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Pylon and open the API token settings at https://app.usepylon.com/settings/api-tokens. Generate a new API token and copy its value immediately, as it is used to authenticate the connector.

setupSteps.title: Verify the token (optional)
setupSteps.body: Confirm the token works by calling the Pylon API `GET /me` endpoint (base URL https://api.usepylon.com) with the header `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. A successful response returns the organization associated with the token and confirms the token is valid.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credential
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's api_token field to the API token you generated. This is the required secret the connector uses to authenticate every request via the `Authorization: Bearer` header.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date to bound how far back data is read. Leave it unset to use the connector's default range. This field is not required.
